Operation 'getEzStat' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary was unclear about the GET method distinction. Description lacked context about auto-creation and notifications that are important for AI agents to understand feature parity with POST. Enhanced to clarify when to use GET variant and include relevant operational details.

data.current_summary: Post stat via EZ API (GET method)
data.current_description: Post a stat using GET request parameters. Functionally identical to the POST method.
data.summary_suggestion: Submit stat via EZ API using GET parameters
data.description_suggestion: Post a stat to StatHat using GET request parameters with the EZ API. Functionally identical to the POST method—stats are created on the fly if they don't exist, and the account owner receives an email notification for new stats. Use this method when POST is not available.
data.reasoning: Original summary was unclear about the GET method distinction. Description lacked context about auto-creation and notifications that are important for AI agents to understand feature parity with POST. Enhanced to clarify when to use GET variant and include relevant operational details.

message: Operation 'getClassicCounter' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary was unclear about the GET method distinction. Description was too brief and omitted important context about signed keys and pre-creation requirement. Enhanced to clarify security mechanism, prerequisites, and use case for GET variant.

data.current_summary: Post counter stat via Classic API (GET method)
data.current_description: Post a counter stat using GET request parameters with signed keys.
data.summary_suggestion: Submit counter stat via Classic API using GET parameters
data.description_suggestion: Post a counter stat using GET request parameters with the Classic API. Uses tamper-resistant signed keys for security. Stats must be pre-created on the StatHat website. Use this method when POST is not available.
data.reasoning: Original summary was unclear about the GET method distinction. Description was too brief and omitted important context about signed keys and pre-creation requirement. Enhanced to clarify security mechanism, prerequisites, and use case for GET variant.
